Home › Scholarships › Turkey, Australia
Turkey, Australia

Turkish and Australian Government Scholarships for Migrant Students

Published 24 Jun 20265 min read✍ MigrantScholar Team✓ Verified 24 Jun 2026
Advertisement

Introduction to Government Scholarships for Migrant Students

As a migrant student, you may be wondering what government support is available to help you pursue higher education in Turkey or Australia. The Turkish and Australian governments offer various scholarships to migrant students, providing financial assistance to help cover tuition fees, living expenses, and other study-related costs. These scholarships are designed to support the educational and career goals of migrant students, promoting cultural diversity and academic excellence in both countries.

Who Qualifies for Government Scholarships

To be eligible for government scholarships in Turkey or Australia, migrant students must hold a valid visa that falls under one of the following categories:

* Subclass 500 (Student) visa

* Subclass 457 (Temporary Work (Skilled)) visa

* Subclass 482 (Temporary Skilled Work) visa

* Subclass 485 (Temporary Graduate) visa

* Temporary Protection visa (TPV)

* Safe Haven Enterprise visa (SHEV)

* Humanitarian visa

Available Scholarships for Migrant Students

The following scholarships are available to migrant students in Turkey and Australia:

Turkey Scholarships

The Turkish government offers various scholarships to international students, including migrant students. Some of the notable scholarships include:

* Name: Türkiye Scholarships

* Coverage amount: Full tuition fee waiver, monthly stipend, and accommodation

* Eligibility: Migrant students who have been accepted into a Turkish university

* Deadline: February 20, 2026

* Official website: https://www.turkiyeburslari.gov.tr/

Australia Scholarships

The Australian government also offers several scholarships to migrant students, including:

* Name: Australia Awards Scholarships

* Coverage amount: Full tuition fee waiver, living expenses, and return airfare

* Eligibility: Migrant students who have been accepted into an Australian university

* Deadline: April 30, 2026

* Official website: https://www.australiaawards.gov.au/

* Name: Research Training Program (RTP) Scholarships

* Coverage amount: Tuition fee waiver and living stipend

* Eligibility: Migrant students who have been accepted into a research program at an Australian university

* Deadline: October 31, 2026

* Official website: https://www.education.gov.au/research-training-program

* Name: Australian Government Research Training Program (AGRTP) Scholarships

* Coverage amount: Tuition fee waiver and living stipend

* Eligibility: Migrant students who have been accepted into a research program at an Australian university

* Deadline: August 31, 2026

* Official website: https://www.uq.edu.au/grad-school/scholarships

* Name: International Postgraduate Research Scholarship (IPRS)

* Coverage amount: Tuition fee waiver and living stipend

* Eligibility: Migrant students who have been accepted into a research program at an Australian university

* Deadline: October 31, 2026

* Official website: https://www.education.gov.au/international-postgraduate-research-scholarship

Application Steps for Government Scholarships

To apply for government scholarships in Turkey or Australia, migrant students should follow these steps:

  • Check the eligibility criteria for the scholarship

  • Gather all required documents, including academic transcripts and language proficiency test results

  • Submit an application to the university or institution

  • Apply for a visa, if required

  • Complete and submit the scholarship application form

  • Provide additional documentation, such as a personal statement and letters of recommendation

  • Wait for the application to be processed and a decision to be made

  • Accept the scholarship offer, if successful

Documents Checklist for Scholarship Applications

Migrant students should ensure they have the following documents ready when applying for government scholarships:

* Academic transcripts

* Language proficiency test results (e.g. IELTS or TOEFL)

* Passport

* Visa (if applicable)

* Personal statement

* Letters of recommendation

* Proof of acceptance into a university or institution

* Other relevant documents, such as a CV or research proposal

Frequently Asked Questions about Government Scholarships

  • What is the eligibility criteria for government scholarships in Turkey and Australia?

The eligibility criteria for government scholarships in Turkey and Australia vary, but generally, migrant students must hold a valid visa and have been accepted into a university or institution.

  • How do I apply for a government scholarship in Turkey or Australia?

Migrant students can apply for government scholarships by submitting an application to the university or institution, and then completing and submitting the scholarship application form.

  • What documents do I need to apply for a government scholarship?

Migrant students will need to provide various documents, including academic transcripts, language proficiency test results, and a personal statement.

  • How long does it take to process a scholarship application?

The processing time for scholarship applications can vary, but migrant students can expect to wait several weeks or months for a decision.

  • Can I apply for multiple scholarships at the same time?

Yes, migrant students can apply for multiple scholarships at the same time, but they should ensure they meet the eligibility criteria for each scholarship.

  • How much money can I receive from a government scholarship?

The amount of money that migrant students can receive from a government scholarship varies, but it can cover tuition fees, living expenses, and other study-related costs.

  • Do I need to repay a government scholarship?

Generally, government scholarships do not need to be repaid, but migrant students may be required to work in the country for a certain period of time after completing their studies.

  • Where can I find more information about government scholarships in Turkey and Australia?

Migrant students can find more information about government scholarships in Turkey and Australia on the official government websites, such as the Turkish Ministry of Education and the Australian Government Department of Education.

For more information about government scholarships in Turkey and Australia, migrant students can visit the official government websites, such as Turkish Ministry of Education and Australian Government Department of Education.

To learn more about scholarships and other opportunities for migrant students, visit MigrantScholar.com blog
